title: pulicaria monocephala
intro: Pulicaria monocephala, a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, is a member of the Asteraceae family, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a shrub-like plant with a single flower head, which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant has been reported to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and soothing respiratory problems. Pulicaria monocephala can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and flowers can be used to make a poultice or ointment for topical application.
